description: Terms, licence status and endpoint identity for every source the Stage-4 acquisition layer is permitted to touch. This ledger is NOT a scoring parameter and is deliberately not part of the method bundle hash (method_config.METHOD_FILES); its own file hash travels in every acquisition manifest instead (`source_ledger_sha256`), so a change to a source's terms moves the manifest, not the science. A source that is not listed here cannot be fetched.
rules: A source may not declare itself free. Every acquired record carries the terms URL recorded here.
reuse_only sources are NEVER fetched by Stage 4. Their records come from the admitted Stage-3 bundle, verbatim, or they do not exist.
Raw bytes are cached OUTSIDE Git under a caller-supplied run root. Git holds synthetic fixtures only.
No source in this ledger supplies logD7.4 or most-basic pKa. CNS-MPO stays incomplete rather than fabricated.

pubchem.license: NCBI usage policy: no NCBI restriction is placed on molecular data; third-party rights may exist in individual records.
pubchem.release_note: PUG REST carries no global database release. Identity is bound by CID + accessed_at_utc + raw_sha256, never by a release string this source does not emit.
permitted_use: active-moiety CID, InChIKey, InChI, canonical structure
PubChem-computed descriptors that PubChem actually reports
forbidden_use: logD7.4 — PubChem does not compute it; XLogP3 is a logP, not a logD, and is never substituted for one
most-basic pKa — PubChem PUG REST does not supply it
PUG-View annotation text as evidence (it inherits source-specific third-party terms)

rxnorm.license: NLM RxNorm terms of service (attribution; RxNorm carries source-vocabulary restrictions for some content).
rxnorm.release_note: RxNav reports its RxNorm version; it is recorded verbatim when the endpoint returns one.
forbidden_use: any potency, exposure, safety or approval claim

dailymed.license: NO BLANKET LICENCE VERIFIED. DailyMed publishes no blanket licence statement on its web-services page, and some SPL content visibly carries third-party copyright. DailyMed also warns that in-use labelling may differ from current FDA-approved labelling and is not reviewed by NLM.
dailymed.release_note: Per-SPL: setid + spl_version + effective_time. There is no global release.
dailymed.redistribution_note: Live label bytes are cached under the run root and hashed. They are never committed: Git holds synthetic, labelled SPL fixtures only.
permitted_use: current in-use label identity (setid, spl_version, effective_time, active-moiety UNII)
labelled safety sections, parsed by analysis/label_adapters.py, bound to the exact response hash
forbidden_use: treating an in-use label as the current FDA-approved label without a Drugs@FDA cross-check
selecting an arbitrary first hit among products or versions

openfda.license: openFDA is generally CC0, WITH marked exceptions where third-party rights are asserted. Data are unvalidated; the original response and its disclaimer are retained.
openfda.release_note: The response meta block carries `last_updated`; it is recorded verbatim as the source release.
permitted_use: machine-readable label fields and their application identifiers
forbidden_use: treating an openFDA field as validated by FDA
dropping a per-record third-party-rights exception when one is marked

chembl.license: CC BY-SA 3.0 (share-alike applies to redistributed derivatives).
chembl.fetch_note: Stage 3 already acquired, hashed and released its ChEMBL records. Stage 4 reuses those exact records and hashes from the admitted Stage-3 bundle. Re-querying would produce a second, unreconciled copy of the same evidence under a different release — so it is refused, not merely discouraged.

uniprot.fetch_note: Same rule as ChEMBL. UniProt establishes target identity only — never drug direction, potency, safety or disease benefit.

clinicaltrials_gov.reason: Not called public domain here. ClinicalTrials.gov's own terms assert third-party rights and international copyright caveats, so it may not be relabelled US public domain. No Stage-4 adapter exists.

faers.reason: If ever added, FAERS is SIGNAL evidence only: it cannot establish incidence, causality or safety.

drugbank.reason: No valid public licence has been established for this project. DrugBank is not queried, not parsed, and no drugbank_id is populated on any public-only production path.
